sources.label: Sohu - Keli Sensing FY2025/Q1-2026 earnings briefing recap (May 29, pre-window; robot force sensors >1,500 units sold 2025, 70+ sampling customers, small-batch to volume transition)
Stockstar - June 1 fund-flow note (net main-capital selling, stock -3.12% that day; nothing fundamental)
Sina Finance - 603662 company announcements list (no new filings June 6-9)

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's verdict already promoted Keli to the top of the robotics alpha list on the under-ownership plus commercialization-evidence case; no new filings or contracts surfaced June 6-9 and today's +1.79% is benign drift, so the setup is unchanged at the current rank. Small positive delta because the alpha case ages well: YTD is still only +0.75%, meaning the force-sensing rerating the thesis anticipates has not yet been paid out.
verdicts.bull: Pure-play force/torque sensing leverage to humanoids at a $2.9B cap with essentially zero YTD rerating; May 29 briefing confirmed >1,500 robot force sensors sold in 2025 and 70+ customers sampling, with six-axis F/T, tactile and IMU lines moving from small-batch toward volume.
verdicts.neutral: Robot sensors remain a small revenue slice of a legacy strain-gauge/weighing business; until a named humanoid OEM design win or volume order is disclosed, the stock trades on flow and sector sentiment, as the June 1 -3.1% fund-flow day showed.
verdicts.bear: Six-axis force sensing is getting crowded (Sunrise Instruments, Donghua Testing, others) and price competition could compress margins before volume arrives; a humanoid-sentiment unwind in A-shares would hit it regardless of execution.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: A disclosed volume order or named design win with a humanoid OEM (Kepler stake and 70+ sampling customers are the funnel), or H1-2026 results showing robot sensor revenue inflecting.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Sampling-to-volume conversion stalls or pricing erodes as six-axis competition intensifies, leaving the robotics narrative unfunded by earnings.
